class.name: ParticleSolver
class.brief_description: GDExtension particle solver for real-time Chladni pattern simulation.
class.description:
Simulates particle motion on a square Chladni plate driven by a beam eigenmode superposition:
[code]ψ(x,y) = X_i(x)·X_j(y) + sign · X_j(x)·X_i(y)[/code]
where [code]X_k[/code] are free-edge beam eigenfunctions (symmetric for odd [i]k[/i], antisymmetric for even [i]k[/i]). Each particle performs a random walk whose step size scales with the local vibration amplitude [code]|ψ|[/code], plus a drift toward nodal lines (where [code]ψ = 0[/code]) weighted by [param drift_speed].
Particles that wander outside [0,1]² are reflected back (specular reflection). A fraction [param respawn_rate] of particles are randomly repositioned each frame to seed new nodal lines.
Typical usage (see [code]control.gd[/code]): [codeblock] var solver = ParticleSolver.new() solver.resize(particle_count) solver.set_beam_lam(beam_eigenvalues) solver.set_beam_coeff(beam_coefficients) solver.set_zoom(zoom) # ... each frame: solver.update(delta, b_i, b_j, b_sign, step_scale, drift_speed, respawn_rate) var positions = solver.get_positions() [/codeblock]

method.name: resize
method.description: Resizes the particle buffer to [param count] and randomizes all positions in [code][0,1]²[/code]. Call this once during initialization, or whenever [param count] changes.

method.name: set_beam_lam
method.description: Sets the beam eigenvalue table [code]λ_k[/code] for [i]k = 0, 1, …, K-1[/i]. These are the roots of the free-edge beam characteristic equations solved via Newton's method. The array is indexed by beam order in [method update]; pass the same table computed on the GDScript side.

method.name: set_beam_coeff
method.description:
Sets the beam coefficient table [code]C_k[/code] for [i]k = 0, 1, …, K-1[/i]. These normalize the beam functions so that [code]X_k(0.5) ≈ 1[/code]:
- Odd [i]k[/i] (symmetric): [code]C_k = cos(λ_k/2) / cosh(λ_k/2)[/code] - Even [i]k[/i] (antisymmetric): [code]C_k = sin(λ_k/2) / sinh(λ_k/2)[/code]

method.name: set_zoom
method.description: Sets the zoom factor applied to the particle coordinate space. Particle positions in [code][0,1]²[/code] are mapped to [code]x·z - z/2[/code] before evaluating the beam functions, so [param z] controls how many eigenmode wavelengths are visible. Larger values reveal higher-order detail.

method.name: get_zoom
method.description: Returns the current zoom factor. See [method set_zoom].

method.name: get_positions
method.description: Returns the current particle position buffer ([code]PackedVector2Array[/code] of length [code]particle_count[/code]). Each component is in [code][0,1][/code]. This is intended to be consumed by a rendering node (e.g. an [Image]-based [TextureRect]) each frame.

method.name: update
method.description:
Advances the simulation by one time step.
Each particle computes the local vibration amplitude [code]vib = |ψ(x,y)|[/code] and: - Takes a random step proportional to [code]vib · step_scale[/code] (Brownian exploration). - If [code]vib > 0.001[/code], drifts toward the nearest nodal line using the signed gradient of [code]ψ[/code], weighted by [code]drift_speed · delta · vib[/code]. The sign of [code]ψ[/code] determines drift direction.
[param b_i] and [param b_j] select the beam eigenfunction orders used in the superposition. [param b_sign] must be [code]+1[/code] or [code]-1[/code]; it controls whether the cross terms add or subtract. [param step_scale] scales the random walk step size. Typical range: [code]0.01 – 0.05[/code]. [param drift_speed] controls how strongly particles are pulled to nodal lines. Typical range: [code]0.1 – 1.0[/code]. [param respawn_rate] is the fraction of particles randomly repositioned per frame. Typical range: [code]0.0 – 0.05[/code].
Returns the updated particle position buffer (same array as [method get_positions]).
